text: 2,2,3,3,4,4-Hexafluoropentane-1,5-diol (HFPD) is a biodegradable ferroelectric molecular crystal with large piezoelectric response. It exhibits strong piezoelectricity with a piezoelectric coefficient d33 reaching ~138 pC/N without poling, and a piezoelectric voltage constant g33 of ~2450 × 10−3 Vm/N. The crystal adopts polar crystal symmetry with P1 space group at room temperature and exhibits ferroelectricity, pyroelectricity, and piezoelectricity. HFPD molecules are connected through O−H···O hydrogen bonding interactions forming a 2D hydrogen bond layer, which induces decent solubility and results in biodegradability. The anisotropy of Young's modulus comes from the ordered arrangement of 2D hydrogen bond network and fluorine atoms arranged on the chain, which might be an important reason for the large piezoelectric coefficient. HFPD-PVA composite films exhibit excellent flexibility, high biocompatibility, and piezoelectricity with d33 of 34.3 pC/N.
experimental_method: Berlincourt method - Direct measurements of piezoelectric coefficients d33 on different crystal planes. PFM (piezoresponse force microscopy) measurements confirmed ferroelectricity and piezoelectricity. P-E hysteresis loops verified ferroelectricity.
computational_method: DFT calculations of elastic constants and piezoelectric coefficients
property_ref_title: Biodegradable ferroelectric molecular crystal with large piezoelectric response
